## [2.10.0](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/compare/v2.9.0...v2.10.0) (2026-05-19)

2.10.0 lands **FIDO2 hardware security keys**, the **multi-account Manage dialog**, and a redesigned **full-window screen-share picker**, alongside fixes for MCAS auth, SharePoint concurrent downloads, and the ozone x11 default. Most new features are opt-in via config; only the screen-share picker is on by default.

### How to enable each new feature

Add the relevant block to your `config.json`:

```json
{
  "auth": { "webauthn": { "enabled": true } },
  "multiAccount": { "enabled": true },
  "download": { "enabled": true },
  "notifications": { "timeoutType": "never" }
}
```

- **`auth.webauthn.enabled`** — FIDO2 hardware security keys. Linux only, requires the `fido2-tools` system package (`apt install fido2-tools` / `dnf install fido2-tools` / `pacman -S libfido2`). Silent no-op on macOS and Windows.
- **`multiAccount.enabled`** — multi-account profile manager (Add / Switch / rename / remove). Mutually exclusive with `auth.intune.enabled`; if both are true, multi-account is auto-disabled with a startup warning.
- **`download.enabled`** — master switch for download completion toast and taskbar progress. Once on, sub-flags `notifyOnDownloadComplete`, `showProgressBar`, and `showTitlePrefix` each default `true` and can be turned off individually.
- **`notifications.timeoutType: "never"`** — persistent notifications on Linux and Windows (workaround for GNOME and other desktops that auto-clear). Choices are `default` or `never`.

Always-on this release (no config needed): the new screen-share picker overlay, the SharePoint QUIC fix, the MCAS proxy cookie-domain fix, telemetry beacon blocking, and log-noise cleanup.

## [2.9.0](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/compare/v2.8.1...v2.9.0) (2026-05-06)

> **Multi-account is a work-in-progress preview.** The new `multiAccount.enabled` flag (off by default) lets you keep separate Teams sessions in the same window. The Add-profile dialog and the Profiles → Switch-to menu work end-to-end in this release, but the Manage-profiles dialog, the visible top-right switcher pill, and `Ctrl+Shift+1…5` shortcuts are still ahead. Useful to test, but expect rough edges and follow [#2495](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2495) for ongoing progress.


### Features

* **config:** allow dismissing startup warnings with "Don't show again" ([#2477](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2477)) ([abb7522](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/abb7522012707853500bbd1a09eebfecef161f1b))

## What's Changed in v2.8.1

### 🚀 New Features

- Add Vylen Linux to distribution packages and fix badge formatting - by @CelestifyX (#2405)
- Add Claude automation hooks for linting and PII protection - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2424)
- Add trial of simili-bot for duplicate issue detection - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2438)
- Add workflow to backfill simili-bot Qdrant index - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2440)
- Add MQTT implementation plan for incoming call events - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2445) ([#2370](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2370))
- Add Wayland GPU auto-disable troubleshooting guide - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2461) ([#2410](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2410), [#2459](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2459))

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

- Fix: Resolve high-severity lodash-es and serialize-javascript vulnerabilities in docs-site - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2402)
- Fix call status detection when hanging up from popup window - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2406) ([#2358](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2358))
- Update dependencies, including Electron, and resolve follow-redirects CVE - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2420)
- Fix: Unbreak cross-distro smoke test by correcting AppImage download and checks - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2422)
- Update DOMPurify to 3.4.0 for security and bug fixes (#2427)
- Fix: Pin Docusaurus v4 future flags for docs-site build compatibility - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2429)
- Fix: Prevent camera resolution override from blurring screen shares - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2437) ([#2432](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2432))
- Fix: Preserve Teams shell when joining meetings and add return path - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2439) ([#2322](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2322))
- Fix simili-bot seeder workflow to download the release binary directly - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2441)
- Fix simili-bot seeder flags for v0.2.0-rc.1 - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2442)

## New Configuration Options

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| `media.camera.resolution.enabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Enable camera resolution control |
| `media.camera.resolution.mode` | `string` | `"remove"` | Resolution mode: `"remove"` removes Teams' constraints allowing native camera resolution, `"override"` sets specific width/height |
| `media.camera.resolution.width` | `number` | - | Target width when mode is `"override"` |
| `media.camera.resolution.height` | `number` | - | Target height when mode is `"override"` |
| `media.camera.autoAdjustAspectRatio.enabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Fixes camera video stretching when moving Teams between monitors with different orientations |

## Deprecated Configuration Options

The following options have been moved to nested structures. The old names still work but will show deprecation warnings:

| Old Option | New Option |
|------------|------------|
| `ssoInTuneEnabled` | `auth.intune.enabled` |
| `ssoInTuneAuthUser` | `auth.intune.user` |
| `screenSharingThumbnail` | `screenSharing.thumbnail` |
| `screenLockInhibitionMethod` | `screenSharing.lockInhibitionMethod` |
| `disableAutogain` | `media.microphone.disableAutogain` |
| `videoMenu` | `media.video.menuEnabled` |

See [Configuration Reference](https://ismaelmartinez.github.io/teams-for-linux/configuration) for full documentation.

openIssues.title: [Feat]: Diagnostics cleanup: renderer error logging, dock icon, log noise (from ELECTRON_DEBUG_LOG=true session)

labels.name: ready
labels.description: There is a fix in develop branch (and snap edge). Soon to be released

openIssues.body:
Findings from analysing the console output of a fresh `ELECTRON_DEBUG_LOG=true npm start` session. None block usage, but they degrade diagnostics or polish, and a couple are correctness/security bugs we hadn't caught.

## Findings

- [x] **Renderer rejection forwarder logs `[object Object]` / `"undefined"`** — `app/browser/preload.js:437` calls `String(reason)` when the rejection reason has no `.message`, which produces the literal `"[object Object]"` for plain objects and `"undefined"` for empty rejections. Teams emits a lot of object-shaped rejections (`{context:"DataLayerClient", code:"InteractionRequired", ...}`), so a large fraction of `[Renderer] Unhandled rejection` log lines carry no diagnostic value. Fix: fall back to a safe `JSON.stringify(reason)` with circular-ref guard, or use the existing `reason` field on the payload (currently sent but ignored in `app/index.js`). — fixed in #2561

- [x] **PII not redacted in renderer error payloads** — `sanitizeRendererLogField` (`app/index.js:369`) only strips URL query strings and length-caps. Teams-internal errors that reach `electron-log` contain `UserId:`, `tenantId:`, `Trace ID:`, `Correlation ID:` values. Teams truncates them to ~8 chars + `...` today, but that's a Teams convenience we shouldn't depend on. CLAUDE.md lists account IDs and SSO info as must-not-log. Fix: add a redaction pass for UUID/GUID-like substrings and known label patterns (`Trace ID:`, `Correlation ID:`, `tenantId:`, `UserId:`). — fixed in #2562

- [x] **macOS dock icon is below the 128x128 platform minimum** — `app/mainAppWindow/index.js:409` hardcodes `assets/icons/icon-96x96.png`. The comment claims a 256x256 default, but no such file exists under `app/assets/icons/` (largest available is 96x96). The runtime warning `Unable to set dock icon for macOS, icon size is less than 128x128, current size 96x96. Using resized icon.` fires every launch on macOS, and the dock icon is an upscaled blurry 128x128. Larger PNGs exist under `build/icons/` (up to 1024x1024) but `build/` isn't bundled at runtime. Fix: add a 256x256 PNG to `app/assets/icons/` and point the dock-icon path at it. — fixed in #2563

- [x] **Auth-recovery window floods the log** — Between the first UPR error and `[AUTH_RECOVERY] Cleared auth state`, the renderer forwards ~120 expected pre-login errors (`login_required: AADSTS50058`, `Error: AuthFailed`, `InteractionRequired`) over ~6 seconds. Recovery works as designed; the noise just drowns out real issues. Fix: suppress or down-level rejections matching those known pre-login patterns while recovery is pending. — fixed in #2564

All four merged for the next release (`ready` label).

## Out of scope (noted but lower priority)

- Build-time `npm warn deprecated` lines (inflight, rimraf@2, lodash.isequal, glob@7/10, whatwg-encoding@2, boolean@3) and "1 moderate severity vulnerability" reported by `prestart` running `npm ci` on every launch. Transitive deps, separate cleanup pass.
- `ResizeObserver loop completed with undelivered notifications` — known harmless Chromium quirk emitted by Teams.

## Plan

Tackle iteratively, one PR per finding, in the order above.

openIssues.title: [Bug]: Screen sharing fails in snap build on Wayland — "Bind context provider failed"

labels.name: awaiting user feedback
labels.description: Waiting for user to confirm they still need this feature

openIssues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug on the Microsoft Teams web app (https://teams.microsoft.com)?

No

### Describe the bug

On Ubuntu 26 with GNOME on Wayland, screen sharing does not work in the snap build of teams-for-linux. Clicking "Share screen" in a meeting does not open the GNOME screen picker — instead, the camera/video gets turned off and the call shows a "video not working" state. Installing the same version from the .deb release fixes the problem entirely.

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

2.9.0

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Install teams-for-linux from the snap store on Ubuntu 26 (GNOME / Wayland session).
2. Confirm snap interfaces are connected: desktop, desktop-legacy, wayland, audio-playback, audio-record, screen-inhibit-control, pulseaudio.
3. Sign in and join any Teams meeting.
4. Click "Share screen".
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1. The GNOME xdg-desktop-portal screen picker should appear.
2. After selecting a screen/window, sharing should start in the meeting.
3. Camera video should remain unaffected.
```

### Debug

```markdown
[84554:0518/125420.985048:ERROR:services/video_capture/video_capture_service_impl.cc:201] Bind context provider failed.
  [84622:0518/125431.556750:ERROR:services/video_capture/video_capture_service_impl.cc:201] Bind context provider failed.
  [84510:0518/125434.650157:ERROR:third_party/webrtc/media/engine/simulcast_encoder_adapter.cc:201] [SEA] StreamContext ctor parent is null
  12:54:44.929 › [Renderer] Unhandled rejection: {
    message: 'screenSharing error: wasSharingOfferred: false, isSharingActive: false',
    ...
  }
```

### Additional context

- Disclaimer I asked Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) to create the ticket contents after the problem was resolved.
- OS: Ubuntu 26, GNOME on Wayland (XDG_SESSION_TYPE=wayland)
- Packages installed: xdg-desktop-portal 1.21.1, xdg-desktop-portal-gnome 50.0, xdg-desktop-portal-gtk 1.15.3, pipewire 1.6.2, wireplumber 0.5.13
- All required snap interfaces are connected (verified with `snap connections teams-for-linux`)
- Launched with `--enable-features=WebRTCPipeWireCapturer` — no change in behaviour
- Installing the .deb from the GitHub releases page (same version 2.9.0) resolves the issue immediately; screen picker appears and sharing works as expected
- Suggests the snap confinement is blocking the xdg-desktop-portal ScreenCast call rather than a code bug

openIssues.title: [Feat]: [PR Offer] Change to allow custom css location to be an array?

labels.name: awaiting user feedback
labels.description: Waiting for user to confirm they still need this feature

openIssues.body:
### Describe the feature

Allow stacking custom CSS - particularly in case of using multiple instances

### Motivation

It would be nice to have a "shared" custom css, and then a "per instance" custom css - without me duplicating that. 

Easiest 'calling' convention for this would be to just have the config allow the cssLocation to be an array of paths.

In the app code, in the two places it uses that - it could check if it's an array, and if so, concatenate all of the files listed. 

### Alternatives

Manually generating/merging across files. 

### Additional context

This looks like a pretty straightforward bit a JS change, would be happy to submit a PR for it if it's likely to be accepted.

### Background

PR #2506 changes the default Chromium backend in our `.desktop` entries from `--ozone-platform=x11` to not providing it. On a Wayland session this means the app runs as a native Wayland client by default instead of being forced onto XWayland.

The X11 force was added in 2.7.4 to mask widespread Electron 38 era Wayland regressions. Electron 41 has shipped meaningful Ozone/Wayland improvements since then, and several reporters have independently validated that `--ozone-platform=wayland` or not providing this works correctly for them now. This is the moment to retest whether we don't need to force x11 anymore.

### What I am asking

I want to ship this default for the next release, but I need as many testers as possible across different setups before I am comfortable. Both "everything still works" and "this regressed" reports are equally valuable. The more compositor, GPU, and packaging combinations I hear back on, the more confident the call is.

### How to test

Grab the PR build that matches your install. Both URLs stay current as new commits land on the PR:

- deb / AppImage / rpm: https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2506#issuecomment-4390021733
- snap: https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2506#issuecomment-4390026028

Launch from the application menu rather than a terminal. The terminal launch path does not pick up the `.desktop` default flags, so testing from the menu is what reflects the actual default for stock users.

### Areas I would like covered

Screen sharing on Wayland (especially on hybrid GPU laptops), maximize and focus-loss behaviour on Wayland, crashes during calls under GNOME Wayland, appTitle / appIcon / awayOnSystemIdle on Wayland, fractional scaling on Wayland, and anything else that worked on 2.8.x but stops working with this build.

### What to report

A short comment with: distro and version, display server (Wayland or X11) and compositor, GPU vendor and driver, package format, what worked or regressed, and whether passing `--ozone-platform=x11` manually restores anything that broke.

Thanks!

openIssues.title: [Feat]: Multi-account profile switcher (ADR-020 tracking)
openIssues.body:
## Summary

Tracking issue for the multi-account profile switcher work led by @jpenberthy, designed in [ADR-020](https://ismaelmartinez.github.io/teams-for-linux/development/adr/020-multi-account-profile-switcher/). Several PRs have already shipped to main and will land in 2.9.0; the rest of Phase 1 plus Phases 2 and 3 are ahead. This single issue is where to follow progress and coordinate; please do not refile separate tickets for the same feature, comment here instead.

The design is settled in ADR-020, this issue only tracks delivery.

## Status

### Phase 1: MVP

Per-profile `WebContentsView`s, top-right switcher overlay, `Profiles` menu with Add / Switch / Manage flows, `Ctrl+Shift+1…5` shortcuts, first-run Profile 0 migration, the six Phase 1 `profile-*` IPC channels, plus the audit-table shared-state migrations.

- [x] ADR-020 published (#2426)
- [x] `multiAccount.enabled` flag and Intune mutual-exclusion check (#2450)
- [x] `ProfilesManager` scaffold and login state migration (#2478)
- [x] Per-profile `WebContentsView` lifecycle + Profile 0 bootstrap (#2483)
- [x] `Profiles` submenu with Switch-to (#2489)
- [x] Add-profile dialog with first-run bootstrap fix (#2496)
- [ ] Manage-profiles dialog (in flight on `feat/multi-account-manage-dialog`, PR pending)
- [ ] Top-right switcher chrome overlay (the visible pill ADR-020 commits to)
- [ ] `Ctrl+Shift+1…5` shortcuts for pinned profiles
- [ ] Screen-preview partition derived from active profile (audit row 2)
- [ ] `CustomBackground` per-instance refactor (audit row 3)
- [ ] Byte-identity-when-disabled E2E smoke test (likely already covered by `multi-account-disabled.spec.js`, confirm)

#### Phase 1 polish discovered post-MVP

- [ ] Screen-share picker handler not bound to profile partition sessions (#2529)

### Phase 2: Background notifications

Per-partition preload notification shim and unread-count tagging, aggregated tray badge, per-profile unread dots, `disableNotifications` and `muted` plumbing per profile.

- [ ] Per-partition preload notification shim
- [ ] Unread-count tagging
- [ ] Aggregated tray badge
- [ ] Per-profile unread dots
- [ ] `disableNotifications` and `muted` plumbing per profile

### Phase 3: Power features

`--profile-id` CLI flag end-to-end, keyboard shortcut to cycle profiles, pinned-profile sidebar (max 5), drag-to-reorder.

- [ ] `--profile-id` CLI flag end-to-end
- [ ] Keyboard shortcut to cycle profiles
- [ ] Pinned-profile sidebar (max 5)
- [ ] Drag-to-reorder

## Where to test

Set `"multiAccount": { "enabled": true }` in your config to opt in. With #2496 merged, the **Profiles → Add profile…** menu entry is wired up end-to-end, so you can add and switch between profiles from the UI. The Manage dialog is ready on `feat/multi-account-manage-dialog` and will land in the next PR; the top-right switcher pill and the `Ctrl+Shift+1…5` shortcuts are still ahead.

## Context

Originally requested in #72, #1656, #1830, #438, and tangentially via #1984 (which was redirected to ADR-010's Quick Chat as the alternative for that specific framing). Those tickets stay closed; subscribers will be redirected here for ongoing progress.

@jpenberthy this is the place to drop status updates, ETAs, and any "stuck on X" notes so the community can follow along and offer to take individual pieces. Thanks again for driving this.

openIssues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug on the Microsoft Teams web app (https://teams.microsoft.com)?

No

### Describe the bug

Screensharing does not hook the screen in wayland, saying "Camera not available" and does not show the screen picker

### What package are you using?

snap

### Version

2.8.0 + 2.8.1

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1.Install Snap
2.Login
3.Join a call
4.Click Share screen
5.Does nothing but show the error
...
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1.Install Snap
2.Login
3.Join a call
4.Click Share screen
5.Show the screen picker
6.Select screen
7.Users on call see what you shared
...
```

### Debug

```markdown
ELECTRON_ENABLE_LOGGING=true teams-for-linux --logConfig='{"transports":{"console":{"level":"debug"}}}'
```

### Additional context

I narrowed down the snap is packaged with some drivers that don't seem super happy with Wayland in the Snap
So I moved to the .deb which got me further but the picker didn't always show up and if it did, screen hooking never works. Users only see my user profile image.
I worked out it's in part due to my hybrid GPU setup with Intel + Nvidia in my laptop
Running the .deb like this in terminal has solved the issue entirely:
```
__NV_PRIME_RENDER_OFFLOAD=1 __GLX_VENDOR_LIBRARY_NAME=nvidia /opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux --ozone-platform=wayland
```

As this is a .deb and isn't managed by apt, I made a bash to help anyone who wants the fix or to keep their deb updated easily
https://github.com/azureskytech/useful_tools/blob/main/teams_linux/fix-teams-for-linux.sh
This just helps you move from snap to .deb, maintain the latest version from this repo and setup the fix for wayland.
The shortcut in your app menu will get updated so you won't have to worry about a thing till an update is released
Feel free to use this while the issue is worked on, as I imagine this is just a quick fix for the issue :)
Hope this helps!

And thanks for all the efforts IsmaelM!

openIssues.title: [Feat]: Custom stickers support
openIssues.body:
### Describe the feature

Add support for custom stickers within the application. The idea is to allow users to maintain a personal collection of stickers (images such as PNG, JPG, or GIF) accessible from within the UI.

### Motivation

Microsoft Teams does not support custom user-defined stickers, which limits personalization and expressiveness in conversations.

Since this project already enhances the Teams experience, adding custom sticker support would provide a unique and highly valuable feature not available in the official client. It would improve user engagement and make communication more fun and flexible.

### Alternatives

Currently, the only workaround is manually uploading images as attachments each time, which is slow and inconvenient.

Another alternative would be using external clipboard managers or scripts, but these solutions are not integrated into the Teams workflow and provide a poor user experience compared to a native implementation.

### Additional context

The feature could provide a dedicated panel or section where users can browse their sticker library and quickly send them in chats. When a sticker is selected, it would be automatically sent as an image attachment to the current conversation.

Optionally, this could include:

- A configurable local folder for storing stickers
- Drag & drop support to add new stickers
- Categorization or tagging for easier navigation
- Recently used stickers section

### Can you reproduce this bug on the Microsoft Teams web app (https://teams.microsoft.com)?

No

### Describe the bug

App suddenly reloads/refreshes page (usually during calls that are 1+ hours long), even if I just logged in an hour or two ago.

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

2.7.13

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Login
2. Join a call or meeting
3. Eventually it will reload - even if I'm presenting.  This usually happens 1+ hours in, but I believe it's happened sooner than that.  I participate in very long troubleshooting sessions, so I run into this multiple times a day.  
4. I don't have to sign-in again or anything, but I do have to wait a few seconds for everything to sync before it will let me rejoin the active meeting from my calendar.
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
That, like the old MS client, it would never reload without me requesting it.  Or, that it would at least only do it when minimized/inactive.
```

### Debug

```markdown
I'm running it today with debug enabled.  Will post anything I can gather during a reload.
```

### Additional context

- My org uses Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps, so connections are redirected though https://teams.cloud.microsoft.mcas.ms.  Mentioning in case it is somehow related.

- Ubuntu 26.04.

- Connectivity is fine.  No other apps drop when the reloads happen.  I have low level music streaming from internet radio all day and it does not drop out when Teams refreshes/reloads.

- I rarely use Teams in the web browser, but it has never done the same thing - even in multi-hour meetings.

openIssues.title: [Bug]: First notification shows title count prefix instead of message content

labels.name: awaiting user feedback
labels.description: Waiting for user to confirm they still need this feature

openIssues.body:
## Description

After the notification lifecycle fix in PR #2329 (merged), notifications now work consistently. However, the first notification received after app launch shows a title count prefix (e.g. "(1) Name") from the currently active conversation instead of the actual message content from the sender.

Subsequent notifications display correctly.

## Follow-up from

This is a follow-up from #2248. The main issue (subsequent notifications stop firing entirely) was fixed by #2329. This tracks the remaining first-notification quirk reported by @SoldierRMB.

## Screenshot

![First notification quirk](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/16b2c5f1-c255-4c03-90ac-4d44fac6e77a)

## Steps to reproduce

1. Launch the app and log in
2. Have a conversation active in the Teams window
3. Receive a message from a different contact
4. The first notification shows the active conversation's title with a count prefix instead of the incoming message content

## Expected behaviour

The first notification should show the sender name and message content from the incoming message, not the active conversation title.

### Describe the feature

Need the correct command line parameter for short meeting links in versions 2.7.3 and later

### Motivation

Hi Ismael,

I just stumbled across the first new style meeting links and found that my jailing script https://codeberg.org/lars_uffmann/teams-for-linux-jailed no longer works with those. I experience the symptoms described in #2140 and I just upgraded teams - the version it shows is:

```
$ firejail --noprofile --private=~/jails/teams-for-linux teams-for-linux --version
firejail version 0.9.74

Parent pid 5897, child pid 5898
Warning: cannot find /var/run/utmp
Base filesystem installed in 0.04 ms
Child process initialized in 8.46 ms
Checking for beta autoupdate feature for deb/rpm distributions
Found package-type: deb
Using user configuration
2.7.7
```

So I assume short meeting links should be supported. However, for the old ones, I was doing a link transformation:
TEAMS_LINK_PATTERN="https\:\/\/teams\.microsoft\.com\/l"
MEETING_LINK=`echo $RAW_LINK | sed "s/$TEAMS_LINK_PATTERN/msteams\:\/l/g"`

Now the new links no longer have the same pattern, and I do not know how you implemented the support for short links: Are they supposed to be provided as the full URL (doesn't work for me -> I get a "sign in" / "join a meeting" dialog that blocks (overlays) my entire teams window and can't be closed), or if not, what is the correct link transformation that my script needs to do?

Thank you for your work!

openIssues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

No

### Describe the bug

I'll be typing in the chat window, and I'll see the blue line under the input window disappear as the message box loses focus.  The app still has focus though.

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

2.2.1

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
It does not happen all the time, but when it does start happening it will occur randomly every five to ten seconds.  

When it occurs, I will just be typing in a conversation with someone and the input box suddenly loses focus - so what I'm typing starts going to null-land (I see no notifications or popups occurring anywhere at the same time).  I have to keep clicking the message box to regain focus every few seconds as the problem repeats.  Then, I might close the app to the tray after that conversation and not see the problem again for the rest of the day.

The problem seems more prevalent when the app's not been running for long - but that could be by random chance.

I have never seen the same thing happen when using the PWA or Microsoft's ancient Linux client.

System:  Ubuntu Questing Quokka (25.10 beta)
Type:  wayland
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
Expected behavior is for the input window to retain focus until I click on something else.
```

### Debug

```markdown
I haven't been able to capture it yet, but I will try to debug after next reboot and add anything I can to the details here.
```

releases.name: v0.1.0 — Repository Strategist
releases.body:
First release of the Repository Strategist — a GitHub App that maintains institutional memory for software projects and uses it to provide doc-grounded triage and strategic intelligence.

## Features

- Multi-phase triage pipeline: deterministic template parsing (Phase 1), vector search + Gemini suggestions (Phase 2), enhancement context briefs (Phase 4a)
- Synthesis engine: weekly cluster detection, ADR drift detection, upstream impact analysis, posted as shadow repo briefings
- Enhancement research agent: mirror issues in shadow repos, context briefs, full research synthesis with safety gates
- MCP server: four tools exposing triage, synthesis, health, and trend data to Claude Code agents
- Event journal: records all repo activity for temporal analysis
- Auto-ingest: re-embeds changed documentation on push
- Per-repo config via `.github/butler.json`
- Live dashboard at `/dashboard`
- Two-layer safety: structural validator + LLM reviewer on all outputs
- Shadow repo approval gates: `lgtm`/`reject` for triage, `research`/`use as context`/`reject` for enhancements

## Infrastructure

Go 1.26.1 on Google Cloud Run, Neon PostgreSQL with pgvector, Gemini 2.5 Flash, GitHub Actions CI/CD with Workload Identity Federation.

## Documentation

- 13 Architecture Decision Records in `docs/adr/`
- Roadmap with six streams in `docs/plans/2026-03-04-roadmap.md`
- Security policy in `SECURITY.md`

openIssues.title: bug: /brief-preview returns [802] as sole upstream_candidate for every query
openIssues.body:
## Summary

`POST /brief-preview` returns `upstream_candidates: [802]` for every query against `IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux`, regardless of the issue being asked about. Observed across all ten simulations in the skill-driven triage pilot (see `docs/plans/2026-05-15-skill-driven-triage-design.md` Follow-ups section).

## Reproduction

```
for n in 2541 2536 2529 2530 2534 2535; do
  curl -sS -X POST https://triage-bot-lhuutxzbnq-uc.a.run.app/brief-preview \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d "{\"repo\":\"IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux\",\"issue_number\":$n}" \
    | jq '.upstream_candidates'
done
```

All six calls return `[802]`. Tested across very different issue topics (custom CSS, deep-link auth, screen-share, downloads, Word/PowerPoint file open, screenshare preview).

## Investigation

`#802` ("Cannot sign in with security key") currently carries the labels `help wanted`, `authentication`, and `ready` on GitHub. It does **not** carry `blocked`.

The actual open `blocked` issues on teams-for-linux are `#2454`, `#2453`, `#2383`, `#2382`, `#2345`, `#2228` (6 total). None of those appear in the `upstream_candidates` slice for any query.

`FindSimilarBlockedIssues` (`internal/store/postgres.go:196-242`) filters by `'blocked' = ANY(labels)` against the bot's `issues` table:

```sql
SELECT ... FROM issues
WHERE repo = $2 AND state = 'open' AND $3 = ANY(labels)
ORDER BY embedding <=> $1 LIMIT $4
```

If `#802` is being returned, its row in the `issues` table must still have `blocked` in the `labels` column. The brief-preview consumer at `cmd/server/brief_preview.go:141-149` then surfaces it as an `upstream_candidate` regardless of semantic distance.

## Likely root causes

Two non-exclusive hypotheses, both worth fixing:

1. **Stale label on `#802` in the `issues` table.** The bot's `issues.labels` for `#802` is probably out of sync with GitHub. Either (a) the `issues.labeled` / `issues.unlabeled` webhook events are not subscribed, or (b) they are subscribed but the handler does not update the `labels` column, or (c) the row was seeded when `#802` was labelled `blocked` and never refreshed. Worth checking which.

2. **No distance threshold in `FindSimilarBlockedIssues`.** The query has `ORDER BY embedding <=> $1 LIMIT $4` with no `WHERE distance < threshold`. Even when the nearest blocked issue is semantically far from the query, it is still returned. The brief-preview consumer treats the result as `upstream_candidates`, which implies relevance; in practice the function returns nearest neighbours irrespective of cosine distance. Per-doc-type relevance thresholds already exist elsewhere (e.g. troubleshooting 0.70, ADR/roadmap/research 0.55, configuration 0.50 — see `CLAUDE.md` Architecture section); blocked-issue cross-reference should have one too.

## Suggested fixes

- **Label reconciliation**: confirm `issues.labeled` and `issues.unlabeled` webhook subscriptions update the `labels` column. If they do not, either add the handler or run a one-shot reconciliation over open issues that have ever been labelled `blocked` (e.g. as a new `cmd/refresh-labels` or a CLI flag on `cmd/seed`).
- **Distance threshold**: add `AND embedding <=> $1 < $5` to `FindSimilarBlockedIssues` with a threshold around 0.45-0.50 (calibrate against the corpus). The brief-preview consumer should drop the slice entirely when nothing crosses the threshold rather than returning a far neighbour.

The label-reconciliation fix is necessary on its own (regardless of `#802`, any stale label in the corpus skews retrieval). The threshold fix makes the function honest about what "similar" means.

## Impact

Cosmetic for now: the `teams-for-linux-issue-review` skill's section 1c warns to ignore `[802]`. But anyone reading `/brief-preview` output via the new MCP tool (other agents, ad-hoc inspection via `cmd/mcp`, future consumers) would be misled. The skill-driven triage pilot's quality notes flagged this across all ten simulations.

## References

- Plan: `docs/plans/2026-05-15-skill-driven-triage-design.md` (Follow-ups section)
- Roadmap: `docs/plans/2026-03-04-roadmap.md` (Open issues for future consideration, skill-driven triage follow-ups)
- Pilot quality notes: `IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux-shadow#97` through `#106`
- Code: `internal/store/postgres.go:196-242` (`FindSimilarBlockedIssues`), `cmd/server/brief_preview.go:141-149` (consumer)

openIssues.title: Simili-bot trial — teams-for-linux (2026-04-20 → 2026-05-20)
openIssues.body:
Weekly-review log for the simili-bot duplicate-detection trial on `IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux`. Full plan, success criteria, and outcome-decision matrix: `docs/plans/2026-04-16-simili-bot-trial.md`.

## Setup

- Installed via `IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux` PR #2438 (workflow) + #2440/#2441/#2442 (seeder iterations)
- Mode: `similarity-only` — related-issue suggestions only, no labels, no auto-close
- Qdrant collection `teams-for-linux-issues` seeded with 1,408 issues on 2026-04-20T10:11Z
- Embeddings: `gemini-embedding-001` @ 3072 dimensions (separate from the triage bot's 768-dim Neon/pgvector index by design — no shared state)
- Trigger: `issues.opened|edited|reopened|labeled` and `issue_comment.created`

## Success criteria (from the plan)

- Precision: >60% of simili-bot suggestions are genuine duplicates/relatives
- Coverage: >=50% of duplicates the maintainer would have flagged manually are surfaced
- Noise: <20% of new issues receive an unhelpful simili-bot comment
- Cost: combined triage + simili-bot usage stays inside the Gemini free tier
- No regression in triage bot's Phase 1 fill rate or dashboard metrics

## Weekly review log

Skim [simili-bot's recent comments on teams-for-linux](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues?q=commenter%3Asimili-bot+sort%3Acreated-desc) once per week, classify each comment as useful / neutral / noise, and note anything surprising.

- [ ] Week 1 review — due 2026-04-27
- [ ] Week 2 review — due 2026-05-04
- [ ] Week 3 review — due 2026-05-11
- [ ] Week 4 review — due 2026-05-18
- [ ] Close-out + `docs/decisions/007-simili-bot-trial.md` — 2026-05-20

## Dashboards to watch

- Triage bot LLM-usage panel: https://triage-bot-lhuutxzbnq-uc.a.run.app/dashboard (cost signal, Phase 1 fill-rate regression)
- Qdrant Cloud dashboard — collection size, query latency
- [simili-bot workflow runs](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/actions/workflows/simili.yml) — failure rate

## Notes / observations

(Add each week's observations as a comment on this issue. Keep it short — useful/neutral/noise counts and any user confusion between the two bots.)

    -   Parse `relationships.DEPENDS_ON` from Compass YAML and resolve referenced services

  - Expose `StructuredLink` type and pass it as optional third parameter to custom markdown templates
  - Surface `customFields` as a markdown table between Links and Dependencies sections
  - Map Compass `typeId` to `service.styles.icon` using Lucide icon names

# @ismaelmartinez/generator-atlassian-compass-event-catalog

## 0.2.1

### Patch Changes

-   53a5fd2: fix: address security vulnerabilities in token logging, path traversal, and XSS
    -   Prevent potential API token leakage via error logs in team fetch failure handling
    -   Sanitize local spec file paths to reject path traversal sequences (`../`) and absolute paths
    -   Sanitize custom field text values from Compass API to prevent XSS in rendered markdown

## 0.2.0

### Minor Changes

-   a547fd4: feat: map Compass DEPENDS_ON relationships to service markdown
    -   Parse `relationships.DEPENDS_ON` from Compass YAML and resolve referenced services
    -   Add a "Dependencies" section to generated service markdown with links to dependent services
    -   Use a two-pass approach: first collect all services, then resolve dependencies between them
    -   Handle missing dependency targets gracefully (log warning, don't crash)
    -   Services without dependencies show "No known dependencies."

## 0.1.0

### Minor Changes

-   d3f5ee0: Modernize and fix core gaps (Phase 1)
    -   Replace local Service type with SDK's Service type for richer metadata support
    -   Map Compass lifecycle, tier, and labels to EventCatalog badges
    -   Map Compass repository links to service repository URL
    -   Map Compass ownerId to service owners
    -   Support updating existing services with overrideExisting option (defaults to true)
    -   Add Zod validation for generator configuration with clear error messages

-   26590b0: Support all Compass component types (Phase 2)
    -   Remove SERVICE-only restriction — all Compass types (APPLICATION, LIBRARY, CAPABILITY, CLOUD_RESOURCE, DATA_PIPELINE, MACHINE_LEARNING_MODEL, OTHER, UI_ELEMENT, WEBSITE) are now processed as EventCatalog services
    -   Add `typeFilter` option to selectively process only specified component types
    -   Add component type badge showing the Compass type (e.g. APPLICATION, LIBRARY) on each service

## 0.0.9

### Patch Changes

-   e6e0f5c: Phase 1: Update dependencies to latest versions

    Updated dependencies to latest versions (Phase 1 of modernization plan):

    -   @eventcatalog/sdk: ^2.0.0 → ^2.9.0 (9 minor versions, no breaking changes)
    -   @changesets/cli: ^2.28.1 → ^2.29.7
    -   chalk: ^5.4.1 → ^5.6.2

    All tests pass with updated dependencies. See UPDATE_PLAN.md for full modernization roadmap.

## 0.0.8

### Patch Changes

-   3c0a302: Updating depencencies and moving to eventcatalog/sdk 2.0.0

## 0.0.7

### Patch Changes

-   06e4c92: Fix docs and update the dependencies

## 0.0.6

### Patch Changes

-   5600c7a: fix chalk version to 4 to avoid ESM breaking changes

## 0.0.5

### Patch Changes

-   7bfa0ac: update dependencies and adding default PROJECT_DIR when not present

## 0.0.4

### Patch Changes

-   85cf3f5: update dependencies and improve documentation

## 0.0.3

### Patch Changes

-   8694a60: Fixing #9 by moving away from Tiles as they don't support external links on bundle objects

## 0.0.2

### Patch Changes

-   92170b3: Initial BETA release

openIssues.title: Verdict-coverage gap: non-TTY callers silently skip the nudge, lifetime coverage at 47.8%
openIssues.body:
## Summary

The delegate-to-ollama metrics reveal a verdict-coverage gap. Interactive callers see the stderr verdict-nudge added in PR #105 and verdict coverage today hit 82% (18/22), but non-interactive callers — scheduled routines, parallel-batch invocations, and CI wrappers — have been silently skipping verdict tracking since PR #140 silenced the nudge when stderr is not a TTY. The PR #140 change was the correct fix for stdout contamination (issue #139), but the trade-off is that any caller running non-interactively now skips the nudge entirely. Lifetime coverage sits at 47.8% (89 verdict rows attached to 187 delegations), with 14 untracked recipe rows accumulating silently across `commit-message`, `summarise-issue`, `pr-review-reply`, and `ci-log-triage` (all pre-2026-05-18, before the nudge existed at all — but the structural gap means new non-TTY untracked rows will continue to accumulate). The current escape hatch `DELEGATE_TO_OLLAMA_FORCE_VERDICT_NUDGE=1` is per-caller and easy to forget.

## Why this matters

The training-loop integrity argument the recipe library was built around depends on every delegation being verdict-tracked so calibration data accumulates. Each MISS reason in the feedback log feeds the next recipe iteration — the 2026-05-21 MISS-themes summarisation that motivated PR #147's enumeration extension was only possible because 20 MISS reasons had been recorded. Untracked rows are a permanent blind spot in the learning pipeline. Without addressing this gap, every automated `delegate.sh` invocation is a wasted training opportunity.

## Suggested fixes

1. Add a `delegate-feedback.sh --report-coverage [--days N]` subcommand that identifies delegate rows from the last N days lacking matching feedback rows. Output: one row per untracked delegation with timestamp, recipe, tier, and model. Callers catch up daily by piping the list into a one-shot verdict prompt loop. Provides immediate visibility into the coverage gap and a remediation path for historical data.

2. Document `DELEGATE_TO_OLLAMA_FORCE_VERDICT_NUDGE=1` explicitly in the SKILL.md "Recipes" section and add routine-author guidance so scheduled routines opt in by default. Lower-leverage and cheaper to implement than fix #1, and propagates the discipline to other multi-agent contexts (Codex, OpenCode, Cursor) without needing per-caller plumbing.

3. (Speculative.) Add a `delegate.sh --require-verdict` option that the caller sets, which writes a synthetic "untracked-nudge" metrics row whenever stderr is non-TTY AND the call had a recipe. The coverage report can pivot on those specifically and distinguish "interactive forgot to verdict" from "routine never had a chance to verdict" — different remediation paths.

## Related

- PR #140 (issue #139) introduced the TTY gate that creates this structural gap; this issue does not propose reverting that, just covering the non-TTY case explicitly.
- PR #147 extended the commit-message recipe enumeration based on tracked MISS data; this issue is upstream of similar future iterations.
- Issue #148 documents a specific summarise-issue MISS uncovered by the first-ever tracked verdict on that recipe today.

openIssues.title: prompts/summarise-issue.md: OMIT-EMPTY-SECTION bypass + comment hallucination on zero-comment issues

labels.name: prompt-pattern
labels.description: Recurring MISS pattern that should graduate to a prompts/ recipe

openIssues.body:
## Task shape

Document two recurring failure modes observed on the `summarise-issue` recipe and propose corresponding recipe-iteration fixes.

## Tier and model

`reasoning` tier → `deepseek-r1:32b` on Ollama. Delegation ts `2026-05-21T16:46:09Z`. Input: full body of issue #134 (zero comments).

## Observation

Verbatim model output:

```
## What happened
- 2026-05-20: User raised the idea of integrating OpenTelemetry for better observability.
- Comment-1: Implementation plan outlined, including opt-in OTLP exporter alongside JSONL.
- Comment-2: Discussed emitting one span per `delegate.sh` invocation with specific attributes.
- Comment-3: Mentioned adding a counter metric and handling feedback verdicts via spans or events.
- Comment-4: Decided to keep JSONL as the primary data source, not replacing it.

## What's blocking
- No explicit blockers stated in the thread.

## What's next
- Implement OTLP/HTTP exporter in delegate.sh with specified attributes and metrics.
- Add handling for feedback verdicts via spans or events.
- Write tests to ensure OTel POST fires correctly under various conditions.
```

**Failure mode 1: OMIT-EMPTY-SECTION rule violated.** The recipe's #1-priority non-negotiable rule says the `## What's blocking` heading MUST be omitted entirely when no blockers were stated, and lists forbidden placeholder substrings (`No blockers`, `no blockers`, `no specific blockers`, `Nothing to do`, `TBD`, `N/A`). The model produced `No explicit blockers stated in the thread.` — same shape but bypasses the literal substring list with the `No explicit blockers` opener that wasn't enumerated.

**Failure mode 2: Factual hallucination of comments.** Issue #134 has zero comments (verified — the JSON `.comments` array is empty). The model invented `Comment-1` through `Comment-4` by treating the issue body's markdown section headings (`## Context`, `## Implementation plan`, etc.) as separate comment entries, conflating body structure with comment metadata.

## Why this is worth filing

These failure modes highlight critical gaps in the current recipe's constraints. The negative constraints for empty sections are defined by literal substring matching rather than semantic patterns, so any synonymous phrasing slips through. The citation rules are ambiguous enough that the model fabricates comment entries from body headings on zero-comment issues. Fixing these ensures higher-fidelity automated issue summarisation and prevents fabricated metadata propagating into downstream comments or status reports.

## Suggested fix

Tighten the forbidden-substring list for the `## What's blocking` section to a substring pattern matching any sentence of the shape `<no | none | not> ... <blockers | mentioned | stated | listed>`, rather than relying on literal opening-string matches. This catches the `No explicit blockers` variant and any other paraphrase that conveys absence semantically.

Add a rule clarifying that `comment-N` citations refer strictly to entries in the input's `comments:` array, and explicitly state that markdown headings inside the issue body must be cited as `body` or `issue body`. This distinction prevents the model from hallucinating comment entries by misinterpreting structural headings within the issue body as separate comment threads.

## Suggested follow-up

After the recipe edit lands, re-run `summarise-issue` on a known-zero-comment issue (this issue, #134, would work as a regression-test target) and confirm both fixes hold. Record the verdict via `delegate-feedback.sh` so the calibration history has the post-fix datapoint.

openIssues.title: bug: delegate.sh verdict-nudge contaminates stdout when callers redirect 2>&1
openIssues.body:
## Summary

`scripts/delegate.sh` writes a one-line verdict-nudge to stderr after every successful call (`delegate: record verdict → bash scripts/delegate-feedback.sh hit ...`). When callers redirect `2>&1` into a file to capture parallel invocations — a natural pattern when fanning N delegations into N background shells — the nudge line ends up mixed into the captured output and has to be stripped before embedding.

## Repro

```bash
bash ~/.claude/skills/delegate-to-ollama/scripts/delegate.sh prose "Write one sentence about X." > out.txt 2>&1
cat out.txt
# delegate: record verdict → bash scripts/delegate-feedback.sh hit (or miss "<reason>")
# <the actual model output>
```

The model output is correct; the nudge line is the contamination. The file then needs a `tail -n +2` or a `grep -v '^delegate:'` strip before it can be used as the slide body / commit message / etc.

## Why it matters

The documented escape hatch `DELEGATE_TO_OLLAMA_NO_VERDICT_NUDGE=1` works but the failure mode is silent: a script that captures stderr for debugging surfaces no warning that the nudge line was injected into the captured text, and the contamination only shows up when a human reads the final artifact. Parallel-invocation patterns (the recipe-worthy shape from the companion `presentation-slide-prose` issue) inherently want one file per delegation, which inherently wants `2>&1`.

## Suggested fix

Have `delegate.sh` detect whether stderr is a TTY via `[ -t 2 ]` and skip the nudge when it isn't, unless the caller explicitly opts in with `DELEGATE_TO_OLLAMA_FORCE_VERDICT_NUDGE=1` (mirroring the existing `_NO_` flag). The metrics discipline intent — a visible reminder for interactive humans — is preserved; programmatic captures stop getting poisoned. Alternative: write the nudge to `/dev/tty` directly rather than `stderr`, which also bypasses any redirection without needing a TTY check.

The first variant is more portable (no `/dev/tty` dependency on platforms that don't expose it the same way); the second is one fewer branch in the script. Either would close this.

## Workaround until fixed

`DELEGATE_TO_OLLAMA_NO_VERDICT_NUDGE=1 bash scripts/delegate.sh ...` or redirect stderr to `/dev/null` (loses the warning for genuine TTY callers but works in a pinch).

openIssues.title: observability: opt-in OTLP exporter for delegate.sh metrics
openIssues.body:
## Context

Per-call metrics are already captured in `~/.claude/skills/delegate-to-ollama/metrics.jsonl` (model, tier, backend, prompt/context/output chars, duration, estimated_tokens_avoided, hit/miss verdicts when `delegate-feedback.sh` is invoked). Today that file is private, on-disk, queried only via `scripts/metrics-summary.sh`. PR #133 just added a `delegate-meta:` stderr line so the assistant can surface model + tokens_local back into the conversation.

The natural next step the user raised in the 2026-05-20 conversation: open the skill to OpenTelemetry so a real observability backend (Grafana Cloud, Honeycomb, Tempo, Datadog) can consume the same data and the assistant + user have something more substantial than a stderr line to look at.

## Scope

Opt-in OTLP/HTTP exporter that fires alongside (not in place of) the existing JSONL append. When `DELEGATE_OTEL_ENDPOINT` is set in the environment, `delegate.sh` posts a span per invocation to that endpoint after the metrics row is written. Off by default, zero overhead when off.

### What to emit per call

One span per `delegate.sh` invocation, named `delegate-to-ollama`, with attributes mirroring the JSONL row:

- `delegate.tier` (string)
- `delegate.model` (string)
- `delegate.backend` (string — `ollama` / `mlx`)
- `delegate.recipe` (string, optional — present only when `--recipe` was used)
- `delegate.prompt_chars` (int)
- `delegate.context_chars` (int)
- `delegate.output_chars` (int)
- `delegate.tokens_local` (int — same as `estimated_tokens_avoided` in the JSONL)
- `delegate.exit_status` (int)
- Span start/end timestamps drive `duration_ms` (so it's not redundant as an attribute)
- Span status: `OK` when `exit_status==0`, `ERROR` otherwise

Counter metric on the side:

- `delegate_to_ollama.tokens_local` (int counter, labels: `tier`, `model`, `backend`, `recipe`) — for "how much have we kept on-device over the last week" rollups in Grafana

A follow-up verdict span (or a span event on the original) when `delegate-feedback.sh` is invoked, carrying `delegate.verdict` (`hit` / `miss`) and the optional reason text — so hit-rate and miss-reason distributions show up in the same dashboard. The feedback row in the JSONL already has the `ref_ts` join key for matching; the OTel emit can mirror that as a span link by trace ID.

## Why opt-in + alongside JSONL, not replacing it

The JSONL is the only surface that survives without a collector running; `metrics-summary.sh`, `audit-metrics.sh`, and the MCP server's `recent_savings`-shaped tool all depend on it. Removing the JSONL would couple the skill to whatever OTel backend the user has reachable, which defeats the privacy framing.

## Implementation sketch

`delegate.sh` already builds the JSONL row via `jq` — the OTel POST is `curl -sS -X POST "$DELEGATE_OTEL_ENDPOINT/v1/traces" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d @-` with a jq-constructed OTLP/HTTP body. Spec: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otlp/#otlphttp. Two callouts:

- The OTLP/HTTP body is a `ResourceSpans` envelope with `traceId` and `spanId` as 16/8-byte hex. Generate via `/dev/urandom | od` (perl handles this cleanly).
- Failures to reach the collector must be silent and non-fatal — the delegation is the primary work, observability is the secondary. Wrap the `curl` in `|| true` and `2>/dev/null`.

Env vars (consistent with the existing `DELEGATE_*` naming):

- `DELEGATE_OTEL_ENDPOINT` — required to enable. Default: unset (exporter off).
- `DELEGATE_OTEL_SERVICE_NAME` — optional. Default: `delegate-to-ollama`.
- `DELEGATE_OTEL_TIMEOUT` — optional. Default: `2` (seconds). Short, because we don't want the exporter to add user-visible latency.

## Out of scope

- Auth headers / TLS / signed requests — leave to the user's collector setup; if their collector needs auth, they front it with a sidecar or a token-injecting curl wrapper. Adding auth to `delegate.sh` would expand the env-var surface considerably for what's an opt-in feature.
- Local OTel SDK as a dependency — adds Python or Go runtime, which contradicts "two bash scripts."
- Forwarding the existing JSONL retroactively — one-shot importer is a separate task.

## Tests

- `test-delegate.sh` cases mocking `curl` to assert the OTel POST fires only when `DELEGATE_OTEL_ENDPOINT` is set, hits `/v1/traces`, carries the expected attributes, and a collector failure does not change the delegation's exit code.

## Related

- PR #133 — `delegate-meta:` stderr line (this PR; the in-console version of the same data)
- ROADMAP Phase 4 — observability/instrumentation track this slots under
